_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: a port of mechanical ventilation when prolonged ventilation is needed in sick patients
to secure the airway bypassing any obstructive pathology in oropharynx/ supraglottis/ subglottis/high trachea
for better pulmonary toileting where patients are unable to cough out their own secretions due to neuromuscular pathologies

preauth.label: Detailed Clinical notes with history, symptoms, signs and indication for procedure

claim.label: Post procedure clinical photograph of the affected part

ppd_preauth.q: Was the detailed Clinical notes with history, symptoms, signs and indication for procedure submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Was Detailed Indoor case papers submitted?
Was Detailed Procedure / Operative notes submitted?
Was the Post procedure clinical photograph of the affected part submitted?
Was the Histopathology report submitted? (In applicable cases)
Was the detailed discharge summary submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes and examination findings do not confirm the need for tracheostomy/tracheotomy.
Detailed Clinical notes with history, symptoms, signs and indication for procedure not submitted for pre-authorization.
Detailed Procedure / Operative note not submitted at claim stage.
Post-procedure clinical photograph of the affected part not submitted at claim stage.
Histopathology report (if applicable) not submitted at claim stage.

clinical_key_pointers: Proceed for Surgery only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical signs, symptoms, examination.

A tracheostomy and tracheotomy are procedures of incising or excising (a small portion of) anterior tracheal wall to facilitate breathing through the cervical trachea instead of normal nasal breathing.
